pattern: Any array with all the evens (in any order) before all the odds (in any order) is accepted, so a single stable pass that buckets each number by parity -- no comparisons, no swaps -- is enough.
hints: You don't need to sort or swap elements in place -- just walk the array once and separate numbers into two buckets by parity.
Build the answer by taking every even number first (in whatever order you met them), then every odd number.
Because any valid grouping is accepted, there's no need to worry about which particular even or odd comes first within its own group.

edge_inputs.description: already all evens, nothing to move
already all odds, nothing to move
odds already appear before evens in the input, needs a full rearrange
smallest possible value repeated, zero counts as even

stress.description: random small integers with a healthy mix of parities
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 5000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
